title: राम कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री राम कवचम् ॥

॥ अथ श्री राम कवचम् ॥

अस्य श्रीरामकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य
ब्रह्मा ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः।
श्रीरामचन्द्रो देवता।
श्रीरामप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः।

शिरो मे राघवः पातु भालं दशरथात्मजः।
कौसल्येयो दृशौ पातु विश्वामित्रप्रियः श्रुती॥ १॥

घ्राणं पातु मखत्राता मुखं सौमित्रिवत्सलः।
जिह्वां विद्यानिधिः पातु कण्ठं भरतवन्दितः॥ २॥

स्कन्धौ दिव्यायुधः पातु भुजौ भग्नेशकार्मुकः।
करौ सीतापतिः पातु हृदयं जामदग्न्यजित्॥ ३॥

मध्यं पातु खरध्वंसी नाभिं जाम्बवदाश्रयः।
सुग्रीवेशः कटी पातु सक्थिनी हनुमत्प्रभुः॥ ४॥

ऊरू रघूत्तमः पातु रक्षःकुलविनाशकृत्।
जानुनी सेतुकृत्पातु जंघे दशमुखान्तकः॥ ५॥

पादौ विभीषणश्रीदः पातु रामोऽखिलं वपुः।
एतां रामबलोपेतां रक्षां यः सुकृती पठेत्॥ ६॥

स चिरायुः सुखी पुत्री विजयी विनयान्वितः।
दीर्घायुस्तं समभ्येति राघवप्रसादतः॥ ७॥

पाताले वा भूतले वा यमले वा नभस्तले।
सर्वत्र सुखमाप्नोति रामरक्षाबलं हरेः॥ ८॥

रामेति रामभद्रेति रामचन्द्रेति वा स्मरन्।
नरो न लिप्यते पापैर्भुक्तिं मुक्तिं च विन्दति॥ ९॥

रामं दूर्वादलश्यामं पद्माक्षं पीतवाससम्।
स्तुवन्ति नामभिर्दिव्यैर्न ते संसारिणो नराः॥ १०॥

पूर्वे रामो दक्षिणे च रामो रामो महेश्वरः।
पश्चिमे रामचन्द्रो मे उत्तरे राघवः स्थितः॥ ११॥

शयने रक्षतु रामो गमने रक्षतु स्थिते।
सर्वकालं सदा रामो रक्षतु मम जीवने॥ १२॥

॥ इति श्री राम कवचम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Rama Kavacham ||

|| Atha Shri Rama Kavacham ||

Asya Shri Rama-kavacha-stotra-mantrasya
Brahma Riship. Anushtup Chhandah.
Shri Ramachandro Devata.
Shri Rama-prityarthe jape viniyogah.

Shiro me Raghavah patu bhalam Dasharathatmajah.
Kausalyeyo drishaw patu Vishvamitra-priyah shruti. || 1 ||

Ghranam patu makha-trata mukham Saumitri-vatsalah.
Jihvam vidya-nidhih patu kantham Bharata-vanditah. || 2 ||

Skandhau divya-ayudhah patu bhujau bhagna-isha-karmukah.
Karau Sita-patih patu hridayam Jamadagnya-jit. || 3 ||

Madhyam patu Khara-dhvamsi nabhim Jambavad-ashrayah.
Sugriva-ishah kati patu sakthini Hanumat-prabhuh. || 4 ||

Uru Raghuttamah patu Rakshah-kula-vinasha-krit.
Januni Setu-krit-patu janghe Dashamukha-antakah. || 5 ||

Padau Vibhishana-shridah patu Ramo-akhilam vapuh.
Etam Rama-bala-upetam raksham yah sukruti pathet. || 6 ||

Sa chirayuh sukhi putri vijayi vinaya-anvitah.
Dirghayus-tam sam-abhyeti Raghava-prasadatah. || 7 ||

Patale va bhutale va yamale va nabhastale.
Sarvatra sukham-apnoti Rama-raksha-balam Hareh. || 8 ||

Rameti Rama-bhadreti Ramachandreti va smaran.
Naro na lipyate papaih bhuktim muktim cha vindati. || 9 ||

Ramam durva-dala-shyamam padma-aksham pita-vasasam.
Stuvanti namabhir-divyaih na te samsarino narah. || 10 ||

Purve Ramo dakshine cha Ramo Ramo Maheshvarah.
Pashchime Ramachandro me Uttare Raghavah sthitah. || 11 ||

Shayane rakshatu Ramo gamane rakshatu sthite.
Sarva-kalam sada Ramo rakshatu mama jivane. || 12 ||

|| Iti Shri Rama Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
मेरे शिर की रक्षा राघव करें, ललाट की दशरथनन्दन, कौसल्यानन्दन दोनों नेत्रों की, विश्वामित्र-प्रिय कानों की रक्षा करें॥ १॥

नासिका की यज्ञरक्षक, मुख की सौमित्रिवत्सल (लक्ष्मण-प्रिय), जिह्वा की विद्यानिधि, कण्ठ की भरत-वन्दित रक्षा करें॥ २॥

कन्धों की दिव्यायुधधारी, भुजाओं की शिवधनुष-भंजक, हाथों की सीतापति, हृदय की परशुरामजित् रक्षा करें॥ ३॥

मध्य भाग की खरध्वंसी, नाभि की जाम्बवान-आश्रय, कटि की सुग्रीवेश, जंघाओं की हनुमत्प्रभु रक्षा करें॥ ४॥

पूर्व में राम, दक्षिण में राम, पश्चिम में रामचन्द्र, उत्तर में राघव स्थित हैं। शयन में, गमन में, ठहरने में — सर्वकाल सदा राम मेरे जीवन की रक्षा करें॥ ११-१२॥

'राम', 'रामभद्र', 'रामचन्द्र' का स्मरण करने वाला मनुष्य पापों से लिप्त नहीं होता और भुक्ति-मुक्ति दोनों प्राप्त करता है॥ ९॥

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीरामकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य ब्रह्मा ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीरामचन्द्रो देवता। श्रीं रामाय नमः बीजम्। सीता शक्तिः। श्रीरामप्रीत्यर्थे रामकवचजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Provides all-round protection through Lord Rama's divine names
Each body part is protected by a specific aspect/name of Rama
Invokes longevity, happiness, sons, victory, and humility
Protection extends to all realms — patala, bhutala, yamala, and sky
Recitation removes all sins and grants both bhukti and mukti
Directional protection — Rama guards all four directions
description: The Rama Kavacham, known in Sanskrit as राम कवचम्, is a divine armor prayer for protection in praise of Lord Rama. This sacred composition is sourced from Traditional Vaishnavite Literature and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to grant longevity, happiness, sons, victory, and humility and provide all-round protection through Lord Rama's divine names. It is also said to protection extends to all realms — patala, bhutala, yamala, and sky. For best results, devotees chant this on Thursday (Guruvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Ram Navami and Dussehra. Comprising 12 verses, it can be completed in approximately 7 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Rama Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Rama Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Protective armor of Lord Rama's names shielding every body part and all directions.

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Rama Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The Rama Kavacham, popularly known as Ram Raksha Stotra, was composed by Sage Budha Kaushika (identified by tradition as another name of Sage Vishvamitra). According to the phalashruti, the verses were revealed to him by Lord Shiva in a dream.

seoFaqs.question: How many verses are in the Ram Raksha Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ram Raksha Stotra contains 38 verses (some recensions count up to 40 with variations in the introductory and concluding verses). It is composed primarily in the Anushtup chhanda of 32 syllables per verse.

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of chanting Ram Raksha Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Known as Vajrapanjara Kavach (the diamond-cage armor), the stotra protects each part of the body, invokes fearlessness, victory over enemies, removal of negative energies, health, prosperity, and ultimately liberation. The phalashruti promises that no calamity can touch one who recites it daily.

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Vajrapanjara?
seoFaqs.answer: Vajrapanjara means 'a cage or armor made of vajra (diamond/thunderbolt)'. The Ram Raksha Stotra is called this because it systematically invokes Rama's protection over every limb and organ of the body, creating an impenetrable spiritual cage of divine protection.

seoFaqs.question: When should Ram Raksha Stotra be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional practice prescribes daily recitation, especially on Tuesdays and Thursdays, on Rama Navami, throughout Chaitra Navaratri, on Vijayadashami (Dussehra), and during times of distress, illness, or before journeys requiring divine protection.

seoFaqs.question: Who taught the Ram Raksha Stotra to Budha Kaushika?
seoFaqs.answer: According to the stotra's own opening verses, Lord Shiva appeared to Sage Budha Kaushika in a dream and dictated the verses to him. The stotra is therefore considered Shiva's gift to humanity for invoking Rama's protection.
